==============================================================================
AZ'ARCH -- FULL COMPONENT SPECIFICATION
==============================================================================

Every single component of the distribution, fully expanded -- nothing
collapsed. For each package: what it is (in plain language), which
layer of the stack it sits in, what it depends on, and what depends on
it. This is the exhaustive companion to:
  - SPECIFICATIONS_COMPONENTS.svg   (the at-a-glance layered graph)
  - SPECIFICATIONS_GENERAL.md   (prose + subsystem breakdown)

The plain-language 'purpose' of each component is the official Arch
package description (the %DESC% field from the core/extra/multilib
package databases -- the same text archlinux.org and `pacman -Si` show),
with the upstream project URL. It is read straight from the package data,
so it is real and never drifts. Browse this file interactively with
scripts/view_specifications.sh.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
AT A GLANCE
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  Base                                       Arch Linux (rolling), x86_64
  Desktop                                    KDE Plasma (X11) with KDE Gear applications
  Kernel                                     linux 7.1.4.arch1-1
  Init                                       systemd 261.1-1
  Live-session writable RAM                  4G writable overlay held in RAM
  Packages (full closure)                    1325
    from core / extra / multilib             228 / 986 / 111
  Az'arch-modified / selected / dependency   16 / 288 / 1021
  Deepest dependency chain                   44 hops (leaf -> base)
  Total installed size                       7.61 GiB

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
HOW TO READ AN ENTRY
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  ### name (version)
  purpose:     what it is, in plain language (from the Arch package DB)
  upstream:    the project's home page
  edition:     one of --
                 az'arch  (Az'arch modifies/brands/themes/removes this package)
                 arch-sel (stock Arch, explicitly listed in the manifest)
                 arch-dep (stock Arch, pulled in only as a dependency)
  category:    a single human-language role
  layer:       0 (kernel) .. 6 (leaf apps) -- real dependency depth
  requires:    the packages it directly depends on
  required-by: the packages in the set that directly depend on it
  optional:    optional deps and the capability each one adds

### udisks2  (2.11.1-2)

purpose: Daemon, tools and libraries to access and manipulate disks, storage
         devices and technologies
upstream: https://www.freedesktop.org/wiki/Software/udisks/

edition:  arch-dep (stock Arch, pulled in only as a dependency)
category: Shared library
layer:    6 of 6  (Applications -- top / leaves, nothing depends on these)
repo:     extra    installed size: 7.9 MiB    license: GPL-2.0-or-later LGPL-2.0-or-later

depth:    163 pkgs pulled in below it (transitively); 70 pkgs above rely on it
requires: acl, dbus, glib2, glibc, lib32-libgudev, lib32-polkit,
          lib32-systemd, lib32-util-linux, libblockdev, libblockdev-crypto,
          libblockdev-fs, libblockdev-loop, libblockdev-mdraid,
          libblockdev-nvme, libblockdev-part, libblockdev-smart,
          libblockdev-swap, libgcc, libgudev, polkit, systemd-libs,
          util-linux-libs
required-by: solid
optional:
    udisks2-btrfs: for BTRFS module
    udisks2-lvm2: for LVM2 module
    udisks2-docs: API documentation
    btrfs-progs: for BTRFS support in libblockdev-fs
    dosfstools: for FAT support in libblockdev-fs
    exfatprogs: for exFAT support in libblockdev-fs
    e2fsprogs: for Ext2/3/4 support in libblockdev-fs
    f2fs-tools: for F2FS support in libblockdev-fs
    less: for default pager of the udisksctl dump command
    nilfs-utils: for NILFS support in libblockdev-fs
    udftools: for UDF support in libblockdev-fs
    xfsprogs: for XFS support in libblockdev-fs
